task_completion.description: Verify the agent has navigated to the Segment Editor module in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module selector or panel title displays 'Segment Editor'.

task_completion.description: Verify a new segment named 'SubcutaneousFat' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The segments list in the Segment Editor panel shows a segment labeled 'SubcutaneousFat'.

task_completion.description: Verify intensity masking is enabled with the correct HU range (-150 to -50).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Masking' section (bottom of Segment Editor), 'Editable intensity range' is checked, and the values are set to -150 (min) and -50 (max).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent has painted the segmentation specifically on the subcutaneous fat layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views, a colored overlay is visible covering the peripheral fat layer just under the skin, consistent with the masking limits.

task_completion.description: Verify the segmentation is saved to the specified export path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or file browser is visible, showing the filename 'fat_segmentation.seg.nrrd' being saved to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the 3D Slicer GUI tools rather than scripting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with buttons, sliders, or the paint tool in the Slicer interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to generate the output file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of a terminal window executing scripts to produce 'fat_segmentation.seg.nrrd' without UI interaction.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not copy a pre-made segmentation file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No file explorer windows showing copy-paste operations of a completed .nrrd file from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verified Hounsfield Unit (HU) range for subcutaneous fat is -150 to -50. The VLM must verify these specific numbers are entered in the Masking settings.
